skill.name: apple-ui-design
skill.description: Apple-inspired clean, minimal, premium UI design. Use when building modern interfaces requiring exceptional UX, clean aesthetics, or Apple-like polish. Triggers on: clean UI, modern design, Apple style, minimal, premium, user-friendly, UX.
skill.summary: Apply Apple-inspired typography, spacing, color, and interaction polish to modern interfaces.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution, but the cited lines are CSS code fences. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are false positives from design wording and CSS examples. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution, or network behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown CSS Fences Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but these locations are Markdown code fences containing CSS examples. They are not executable instructions and do not invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged characters are Markdown fences around CSS samples. No shell command, Ruby interpolation, or execution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Text Reported as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithms in descriptive design text. The skill contains UI guidance and CSS examples, with no cryptographic code or security-sensitive algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Apple-inspired UI design. There is no cryptographic function, import, configuration, or code path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: CSS Examples Reported as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reported system and network reconnaissance locations are CSS examples for spacing and borders. They do not inspect the host system, enumerate networks, or access remote services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are static CSS syntax inside documentation. No system calls, network APIs, or discovery commands appear nearby.

content.value_statement: Modern product interfaces often feel crowded or inconsistent. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical Apple-style UI rules for cleaner screens.
actual_capabilities: Applies Apple-inspired typography hierarchy using system font stacks.
Defines light and dark mode color variables for clean interfaces.
Provides spacing values based on a 4px and 8px rhythm.
Suggests card, button, and animation CSS patterns.
Supplies a checklist for touch targets, contrast, width, and motion.
limitations: Does not include complete component libraries or production-ready design tokens.
Does not verify Apple Human Interface Guidelines compliance in detail.
Does not generate assets, icons, or brand-specific visual systems.
Uses general Apple-inspired patterns, not official Apple design resources.

use_cases.title: Polish a SaaS Dashboard
use_cases.description: Refine typography, spacing, buttons, and cards for a cleaner product interface.

use_cases.title: Guide Frontend Styling
use_cases.description: Apply consistent CSS variables and interaction rules while building a modern app.

use_cases.title: Review Prototype Quality
use_cases.description: Check a prototype for contrast, touch target size, spacing rhythm, and visual hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Single Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this screen and suggest Apple-inspired typography, spacing, color, and button improvements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one page or mockup

prompt_templates.title: Create Design Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a compact Apple-inspired token set for typography, color, spacing, surfaces, and motion.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor UI Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Update these components to follow Apple-inspired cards, primary actions, subtle motion, and dark mode support.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Product Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this multi-step product flow for clarity, deference, depth, accessibility, and premium interaction polish.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced UX and visual quality review

output_examples.input: A settings page feels cluttered and hard to scan.
output_examples.output: The response would recommend clearer section hierarchy, wider spacing, quieter secondary text, one primary action, and stronger contrast.

output_examples.input: A landing page needs Apple-style visual polish.
output_examples.output: The response would define typography scale, neutral surface colors, restrained accent use, rounded cards, and subtle fade-up motion.

output_examples.input: A mobile app screen needs a quick UX checklist.
output_examples.output: The response would check 44px touch targets, readable body text, dark mode support, smooth animation, and consistent spacing.

best_practices: Provide screenshots, component code, or clear layout descriptions for best results.
Ask for specific changes to typography, spacing, surfaces, or interaction states.
Pair the guidance with accessibility checks before shipping a production interface.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the guidance as official Apple brand or platform compliance.
Do not apply large pill buttons and glass cards to every component.
Do not skip contrast, responsive layout, and touch target validation.

faq.question: Does this skill create complete Apple apps?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance and CSS patterns for Apple-inspired interface polish.

faq.question: Can it help with existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest updates to typography, spacing, surfaces, buttons, and animation styles.

faq.question: Does it use official Apple design assets?
faq.answer: No. It describes general Apple-inspired patterns and does not include official assets.

faq.question: Is dark mode covered?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes basic light and dark mode color variable guidance.

faq.question: Can it perform accessibility testing?
faq.answer: It provides checklist guidance, but it does not replace automated or manual accessibility testing.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
